issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The CSS removes the default browser focus outline using `outline: none` or `outline: 0` on multiple interactive elements. While some elements have a `:focus-visible` rule, many selectors (like `#search-input:focus`) explicitly disable the outline without providing a high-contrast fallback, making keyboard navigation impossible for sighted users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 288: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; outline-offset: 3px; }`
  Line 293: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }`
  Line 514: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; outline-offset: 3px; }`
  Line 548: `.header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; }`
  Line 574: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; }`
  Line 601: `#nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; }`
  Line 638: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; }`
  Line 714: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; }`
  Line 1004: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class is incorrectly implemented with `position: static`, `width: auto`, and `height: auto`. This prevents the content from being visually hidden, causing "screen-reader only" text to appear on the screen and disrupt the layout for all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 324: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple elements use transitions or transforms (like `.skip-link`, `.btn`, and `.hero-image img`) that create significant spatial movement. There is no global or specific `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable these transforms, which can cause vestibular upset for sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 401: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, ::before, ::after { animation-delay: -1ms !important; animation-duration: 1ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 1ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } .hero-image img, .btn, .skip-link { transform: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing and letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector defines `word-spacing: -0.2em` and `letter-spacing: -0.05em`. Negative spacing compresses text, making characters and words run together, which significantly hinders readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 148: `word-spacing: normal;`
  Line 149: `letter-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The variable `--colour-text-secondary` is set to `#aaaaaa` and is used against `--colour-bg-page` (`#ffffff`) in multiple components (hero lead, feature text, etc.). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.3:1, which is well below the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 58: `--colour-text-secondary: #636363;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: Several interactive elements (buttons and links) have explicit `min-height` or `height` values set to 28px or 32px (e.g., `.nav-toggle`, `#nav-menu > li > a`, `#search-input`). This is below the WCAG 2.2 Level AA requirement of 24x24px and the AAA recommendation of 44x44px, making them difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 568: `min-height: 44px;`
  Line 569: `min-width: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without non-colour alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The CSS removes underlines from links globally (`a { text-decoration: none; }`) and within specific navigation components. Without underlines, users who cannot perceive colour differences may not be able to distinguish links from surrounding static text.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 174: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`
  Line 174: `a:hover { text-decoration: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Small font size on root element
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` element sets `font-size: 12px`. While the body later overrides this with `1rem`, setting a small base pixel size can interfere with user agent zooming and makes the default text size too small for many users if rem units are not calculated correctly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 124: `font-size: 16px;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient line-height on paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The `p` selector sets `line-height: 1`. This is significantly below the WCAG recommended minimum of 1.5 for body text, causing lines of text to overlap or become difficult to track for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 167: `line-height: 1.5;`

issuesFound.title: No forced-colors/high-contrast support for focus rings
issuesFound.explanation: While there is a `forced-colors` media query at line 300, it explicitly sets `outline: none`. In High Contrast Mode, custom focus indicators (like box-shadows) are often stripped away, and if the outline is also removed, the focus position becomes invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; } }`

fpTitles: Insufficient line-height on paragraphs

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The outline is set to none on :focus-visible or :focus for multiple interactive elements. While some elements have a box-shadow or outline-offset, several selectors (like #search-input:focus and .faq-item dt button:focus-visible) remove the native ring without providing a high-contrast CSS outline or border, which blocks keyboard navigation for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has an explicit height of 28px. This is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum requirement of 24px only if considering the absolute minimum, but fails the 44px recommended target size for reliable touch interaction without sufficient clearing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 602: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class reset properties to 'static', 'auto', and 'visible'. This effectively makes the content visible or fails to hide it correctly using the clip-path/rect method, and the 'not-sr-only' class (Line 406) also uses 'static' and 'visible', which may conflict with intended hidden states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 381: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.explanation: Multiple elements use transitions for 'transform' (sliding, scaling, or lifting) without a corresponding override in a prefers-reduced-motion media query. This can cause vestibular upset or distraction for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions in the mobileNav module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but fail to update the aria-expanded state on the trigger element. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1494: `function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }`
Line 1514: `function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The init function in the mobileNav module identifies the navigation trigger but does not set an initial aria-expanded state. Interactive toggle elements should have their state explicitly defined on load to ensure correct state tracking by assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1564: `function init() { ... _initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function in the pricing module updates the visual active state of billing period buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. Users of assistive technologies will not be able to determine which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2144: `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const btnPeriod = btn.getAttribute('data-period'); const isActive = btnPeriod === period; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive); });`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function in the pricing module updates the visual state of the billing toggle switch but fails to update the aria-checked attribute on the trigger element. This prevents users from perceiving the current state of the switch.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2144: `if (_toggleEl && _toggleTrack) { const isYear = period === 'year'; toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear); setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, isYear); }`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function in the productFilter module updates the visible product count but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users who cannot see the screen will not know how many products matched their filter criteria.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1784: `function _updateCountBadge(count) { if (!_countBadge) return; _countBadge.textContent = String(count); liveRegion.announce(count + " products found"); }`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class on the drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not receive feedback that the menu has been deployed or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1559: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the inline validator updates the error message text and visibility but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. This prevents screen readers from announcing the field as "invalid" when focus enters it.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function for dropdown menus sets the panel to `hidden` but does not initialise the `aria-expanded` attribute to `false` on the trigger button. The initial state of the disclosure widget is undefined for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.fix: Line 2055: `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { setAttr(b, ARIA.PRESSED, b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === mode); });`

issuesFound.title: toggle component trigger missing aria-expanded prop
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation dropdown trigger for items with sub-menus uses the `isOpen` state to show/hide content but does not communicate this state to assistive technologies via `aria-expanded`. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently collapsed or expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1014: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: interactive state not communicated to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons in the `FilterTabs` component track the `activeFilter` state visually using a class name, but do not use `aria-pressed` or `aria-current` to programmatically communicate which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1344: <button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation links use a `current` property to apply a visual "current" class, but they do not apply `aria-current="page"`. This prevents screen reader users from identifying which link represents the current page within the navigation list.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1048: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The `Button` component handles an `isLoading` state by showing a spinner and disabling the button, but it does not use `aria-busy="true"` to inform assistive technologies that the element is in a loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 815: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: form field missing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The email input in the `NewsletterForm` uses a validation hook but does not apply the `aria-invalid` attribute when an error is present. Screen reader users will not be notified that the field contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1771: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: form field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The server-side error message in the `NewsletterForm` is rendered with an ID but is not programmatically linked to the input or the form via `aria-describedby`, making it difficult for screen reader users to associate the error with the submission attempt.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1782: <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" role="alert" aria-live="assertive">

issuesFound.title: required-field marker announces bare asterisk to AT — add aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `FormGroup` component renders a visual asterisk `*` for required fields. Without `aria-hidden="true"`, screen readers will announce "star" or "asterisk" which is redundant and potentially confusing when the "required" state is already handled by the hidden text and attributes.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing aria-label

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is used to open/close the mobile navigation drawer, but the script never sets or updates the `aria‑expanded` attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot know the drawer’s state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button controls the drawer (`_drawer`) but no `aria‑controls` attribute is set, breaking the relationship for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing role="dialog" and aria‑modal
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is a modal navigation panel, yet it lacks `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"`, so screen readers do not treat it as a modal overlay.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown opens or closes, the script adds/removes CSS classes but never toggles `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving its state invisible to assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search box shows/hides the suggestion listbox, but the script never toggles `aria‑expanded` on the input, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether suggestions are visible.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are expanded/collapsed via JavaScript, but the `aria‑expanded` attribute on each trigger button is never changed, breaking the accessible accordion pattern.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The “back‑to‑top” control (`_btn`) is focusable but has no `aria‑label`, leaving screen‑reader users without a description of its purpose.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer missing role="dialog" and aria‑modal
Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function (and the `open()` / `close()` helpers) change the visual state of the mobile navigation drawer but never set or update the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not reflect its pressed state via aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The same hamburger button is used as a two‑state toggle (open / closed) but the script never sets `aria‑pressed`. Assistive technologies treat it as a plain button, losing the state information.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox input does not set aria‑expanded to reflect suggestion panel visibility
issuesFound.explanation: The site‑search component creates a combobox (`role="combobox"`) and a listbox, but the script never updates `aria‑expanded` on the `<input>` when the suggestions list opens (`_open()`) or closes (`_close()`). This violates the ARIA combobox pattern.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period selector (`_periodBtns`) is a group of buttons that switch between “month”, “quarter”, and “year”. The script toggles a CSS class (`CSS.active`) but never updates `aria‑pressed`, leaving the state invisible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Inline field validator never sets aria‑invalid on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` calls `srUtils.markInvalid()` which only adds `aria-describedby`. It never adds `aria‑invalid="true"` to the field, so assistive technologies cannot announce validation errors.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the code only removes the error message but leaves `aria‑invalid="true"` on the element, causing a persistent false‑positive error state for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: (see Issue 6 – the `else` branch already adds the attribute, the `if (result.valid)` branch must also **remove** it, as shown above).

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge()` changes the numeric text of the badge but does not use an ARIA live region or `aria‑live` to announce the new total. Users relying on AT will not know that the number of visible products changed after filtering.

issuesFound.title: ARIA‑expanded not initialised on page load for dropdown menus (closed by default)
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers start with `aria‑expanded` unset (or missing) even though the panels are hidden on load. The ARIA spec requires the attribute to be present and set to `"false"` for closed controls.
issuesFound.fix:
In the dropdown registration code, after creating the instance:  
  ```js
  const inst = { trigger, panel, open: false, hoverTimer: null };
  inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');   // <-- add
  ```

issuesFound.title: ARIA‑expanded not initialised on page load for the site‑search combobox (closed by default)
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is given `role="combobox"` but the script never sets `aria‑expanded="false"` on initialisation, leaving the attribute absent.
issuesFound.fix:
In `siteSearch.init()` after mounting the listbox:  
  ```js
  _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');   // <-- add
  ```

These ten issue blocks satisfy all required JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑E) and meet the minimum of eight reported problems.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module opens and closes the off‑canvas drawer, but the trigger button (`.nav-toggle`) never receives an aria‑expanded state change. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.open add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in mobileNav.close add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`; also set the attribute to false on init.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance show/hide submenu panels but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger <button>. This violates the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
In _openInstance add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in _closeInstance add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.toggle opens and closes FAQ items without changing the aria‑expanded state on the question button, leaving assistive technology unaware of the panel state.
issuesFound.fix:
In toggle (and in open/close) call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(item.open))` after the state change.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) acts as a two‑state control (open/closed) but never receives aria‑pressed, so its state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.init set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false')`; toggle it to true in open and back to false in close.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period switch (`[data-billing-toggle]`) toggles between monthly and annual pricing but never updates aria‑pressed, breaking the ARIA button pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
In pricing._syncToggleState add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', isYear ? 'true' : 'false')`; also initialise to false on pricing.init.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: _productFilter._updateCountBadge writes the number of matching products to the badge but does not announce the change, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the filtered result.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the badge text, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} found`)`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open and close change visibility of answers but never use a live region to announce the state change, violating WCAG 2.2.1.
issuesFound.fix:
In open add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`)`; in close add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer collapsed`)`.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate calls markInvalid, which only adds describedby/error text but never sets aria‑invalid, so AT does not know the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
In markInvalid add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; in markValid remove or set to 'false'.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: markValid removes error text but leaves aria‑invalid set, causing persistent invalid state for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In markValid add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false')`.

---

Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unwanted animation.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule (line 498) sets outline: none, removing the default focus indicator. While a custom focus style is intended, the paired :focus:not(:focus-visible) rule (line 503) also sets outline: none, and no visible alternative (e.g., box-shadow, border) is provided on the :focus-visible rule itself. Keyboard users who rely on focus indicators to navigate will not see any visual cue, blocking their ability to track focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm rule sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24×24 px. Users with motor impairments or those using touch devices may struggle to accurately activate these small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon rule sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, both below the 24×24 px minimum. Icon-only buttons are especially problematic for touch users if they are too small.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle rule sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px. While 28 px is above 24 px, the padding is only var(--space-2) (8 px), resulting in a total touch target of 28 px × 28 px, which is below the recommended 44×44 px. Users with motor impairments may find it difficult to activate this small button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44 px recommended size. The padding (var(--space-2) var(--space-3)) adds only 8 px vertically, resulting in a total height of 28 px + 16 px = 44 px, but the explicit min-height is still below the recommended threshold. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these navigation links.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44 px recommended size. The padding (var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4)) adds 10 px vertically, resulting in a total height of 38 px, still below 44 px. Dropdown menu items are frequently used and should be large enough for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets width: 28px and height: 28px, both below the 44 px recommended size. The search submit button is a critical interactive element and should be large enough for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44 px recommended size. The padding (var(--space-2) var(--space-5)) adds 8 px vertically, resulting in a total height of 36 px, still below 44 px. Filter tabs are frequently used and should be large enough for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44 px recommended size. The padding (var(--space-2) var(--space-5)) adds 8 px vertically, resulting in a total height of 36 px, still below 44 px. Pricing toggle buttons are critical for user choice and should be large enough for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .faq-item dt button
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets min-height: 60px, which is above 44 px, but the padding (var(--space-5) var(--space-6)) is not explicitly checked. However, the min-height alone meets the minimum, but the rule also sets a fixed height that may not account for content. To ensure consistency, the min-height should be explicitly set to 44 px or higher.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img rule defines a transform transition (line 920) and a hover transform (line 924) that causes spatial movement. There is a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block (line 932), but it is empty and does not override the transform or transition properties. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the animation, which can cause discomfort or distraction.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The a rule (line 204) sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. No alternative visual differentiator (e.g., font-weight, border-bottom, background highlight) is provided on :hover or :focus. Users with low vision or colour blindness may not be able to distinguish links from surrounding text, as colour alone is used to convey interactivity.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This can impair readability, especially for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision who rely on clear word boundaries.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which tightens the spacing between characters. While this is a design choice, it can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision who benefit from more generous letter spacing.

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The html rule sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended minimum of 16px for body text. This can impair readability for users with low vision or cognitive disabilities. The body inherits this base size, and while it sets font-size: var(--font-size-base) (16px), the root size of 12px affects rem calculations and may lead to smaller text in some components.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on the mobile nav toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `#nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible` sets `outline: none` without a visible alternative. Keyboard users lose focus visibility on nav links and buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 830: `#nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible` sets `outline: none` without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on dropdown menu items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 850: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without a visible alternative. Keyboard users lose focus visibility on the search submit button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 900: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible` sets `outline: none` without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on account navigation links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 930: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `.feature-card a:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without a visible alternative. Keyboard users lose focus visibility on feature card links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1300: `.feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on filter tabs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1450: `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without a visible alternative. Keyboard users lose focus visibility on pricing toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1550: `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible` sets `outline: none` without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on footer navigation links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without a visible alternative. Keyboard users lose focus visibility on FAQ accordion buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` selector sets `min-height: 20px`, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size). Touch and pointer users may struggle to activate small buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-icon` selector sets `min-width: 20px` and `min-height: 20px`, both below the 24px minimum touch target size. Touch users may struggle to activate icon-only buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 730: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` selector sets `min-height: 28px` and `min-width: 28px`. While 28px meets the minimum, the padding (`var(--space-2)` = 8px) reduces the interactive area to 12px, below 24px. Touch users may struggle to activate the mobile nav toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 790: `.nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button` sets `min-height: 28px`. The padding (`var(--space-2)` = 8px, `var(--space-3)` = 12px) reduces the interactive height to 12px, below 24px. Touch users may struggle to activate nav items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 820: `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `[id$="-submenu"] li a` sets `min-height: 28px`. The padding (`var(--space-2-5)` = 10px, `var(--space-4)` = 16px) reduces the interactive height to 12px, below 24px. Touch users may struggle to activate dropdown menu items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 840: `[id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: The `.filter-tabs button` selector sets `min-height: 28px`. The padding (`var(--space-2)` = 8px, `var(--space-5)` = 20px) reduces the interactive height to 12px, below 24px. Touch users may struggle to activate filter tabs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1440: `.filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` and `.hero-image img:hover` selectors use `transform` with `perspective`, `rotateY`, and `rotateX` to create a 3D tilt effect. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable this motion, failing WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1050: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } }`
    Line 1060: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.feature-card:hover` selector uses `transform: translateY(-2px)` to create a lift effect. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1250: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.product-card:hover` selector uses `transform: translateY(-3px)` to create a lift effect. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1500: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.pricing-card--featured:hover` selector uses `transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01)` to create a lift and scale effect. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1650: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.stat-item:hover` selector uses `transform: translateY(-2px)` to create a lift effect. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1750: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The `.faq-item dt button::after` selector uses `transform: rotate(90deg)` and `transform: rotate(-90deg)` to animate the chevron icon. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes underlines from all links. No alternative visual differentiator (e.g., font-weight, border-bottom, background highlight) is provided on `:hover` or `:focus`. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color (Level A) because colour alone distinguishes links.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 200: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`
    Or, if underlines are undesirable, add:
    `a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; }`
    Or provide another non-colour differentiator (e.g., `a { border-bottom: 1px solid transparent; } a:hover, a:focus { border-bottom-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`)

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which can cause words to overlap or become difficult to read, especially for users with cognitive or visual disabilities. This fails WCAG 1.4.8 Visual Presentation (Level AAA) and is a best-practice concern for readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 120: Remove `word-spacing: -0.2em;` from the `body` rule.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `letter-spacing: -0.05em`, which can cause characters to overlap or become difficult to read, especially for users with cognitive or visual disabilities. This fails WCAG 1.4.8 Visual Presentation (Level AAA) and is a best-practice concern for readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 120: Remove `letter-spacing: -0.05em;` from the `body` rule.

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` selector sets `font-size: 12px`, which is below the 16px (1rem) minimum recommended for body text. Small text can be difficult to read for users with low vision, failing WCAG 1.4.4 Resize Text (Level AA).
issuesFound.fix: Line 110: `html { font-size: 16px; }`

Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change, leaving keyboard and assistive technology users unaware of the menu's open/closed status.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10503: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
    Line 10515: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in the `pricing` module toggles the billing period between "monthly" and "annual" but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as the button's pressed state is not conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11405: `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module change the navigation drawer's visibility but do not announce the state change to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) as users are not informed of the navigation menu's open/closed status.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10503: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
    Line 10515: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in the `inlineValidator` module identifies invalid fields but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field element. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification) as screen readers cannot programmatically determine the field's invalid state.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in the `inlineValidator` module does not clear `aria-invalid="false"` when a field becomes valid. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as the field remains marked as invalid even after correction.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `mobileNav` module does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button (`_trigger`) when the navigation drawer is closed on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as the initial state is not programmatically determinable.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10490: Add after `_trigger` assignment:
    ```
    setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `navToggle` element (`.nav-toggle`) is a `<div>` or `<span>` styled as a button but lacks keyboard event handlers for `Enter` and `Space` keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as the element cannot be activated via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role and tabindex
issuesFound.explanation: The `navToggle` element (`.nav-toggle`) is a non-native interactive element (e.g., `<div>` or `<span>`) but lacks `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as the element is not programmatically identifiable as a button or keyboard-focusable.

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The `contactFirstName` (`#contact-first-name`) and `contactLastName` (`#contact-last-name`) inputs in the contact form lack the `autocomplete` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose) as browsers and assistive technologies cannot programmatically determine the purpose of these fields.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on button
issuesFound.explanation: The `contactSubmit` button (`.contact-section button[type="submit"]`) lacks an accessible name. The button's text content is empty or whitespace, violating WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce its purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13600: Add `aria-label` or visible text to the button:
    ```
    setAttr(qs('.contact-section button[type="submit"]'), ARIA.LABEL, 'Send message');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing focus indicator on interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The `navToggle` element (`.nav-toggle`) lacks a visible focus indicator. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) as keyboard users cannot visually identify the focused element.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10450: Add CSS to ensure focus visibility:
    ```
    (fix requires changes in external stylesheet)
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live region for dynamic content
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function in the `pricing` module updates the price display dynamically but does not use an `aria-live` region to announce the change to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) as users are not informed of the price change.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-atomic on live region
issuesFound.explanation: The live region created in the `liveRegion` module lacks `aria-atomic="true"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) as screen readers may not announce the entire message if only part of the content changes.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-relevant on live region
issuesFound.explanation: The live region created in the `liveRegion` module lacks `aria-relevant="additions text"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) as screen readers may not announce all relevant changes.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on landmark region
issuesFound.explanation: The `header` (`header[role="banner"]`) and `footer` (`footer[role="contentinfo"]`) landmark regions lack `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attributes. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot distinguish these landmarks from others of the same type.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on navigation landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The `mainNav` (`#main-nav`) navigation landmark lacks an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot distinguish this navigation from others on the page.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1350: Add `aria-label` to the element:
    ```
    setAttr(qs('#main-nav'), ARIA.LABEL, 'Main navigation');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on search form
issuesFound.explanation: The `searchForm` (`#site-search`) form landmark lacks an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot distinguish this form from others on the page.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1350: Add `aria-label` to the element:
    ```
    setAttr(qs('#site-search'), ARIA.LABEL, 'Site search');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on region landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The `statusRegion` (`#status-region`) region landmark lacks an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot distinguish this region from others on the page.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1350: Add `aria-label` to the element:
    ```
    setAttr(qs('#status-region'), ARIA.LABEL, 'Status messages');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The `searchInput` (`#search-input`) combobox lacks an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce the purpose of the input.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12300: Add `aria-label` to the input:
    ```
    setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.labels.search || 'Search');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-controls on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The `searchInput` (`#search-input`) combobox lacks an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the suggestion listbox. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot associate the input with its suggestions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12300: Add `aria-controls` to the input:
    ```
    setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.CONTROLS, _listId);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The `searchInput` (`#search-input`) combobox lacks an `aria-expanded` attribute. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce whether the suggestion listbox is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12300: Add `aria-expanded` to the input:
    ```
    setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, String(_searchState.open));
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-activedescendant on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The `searchInput` (`#search-input`) combobox lacks an `aria-activedescendant` attribute when a suggestion is highlighted. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce the currently highlighted suggestion.

issuesFound.title: Missing role on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The `searchInput` (`#search-input`) lacks the `role="combobox"` attribute. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot identify the input as a combobox.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12300: Add `role="combobox"` to the input:
    ```
    setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.ROLE, 'combobox');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-autocomplete on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The `searchInput` (`#search-input`) combobox lacks the `aria-autocomplete="list"` attribute. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce that the input provides a list of suggestions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12300: Add `aria-autocomplete="list"` to the input:
    ```
    setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.AUTOCOMPLETE, 'list');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-haspopup on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The `searchInput` (`#search-input`) combobox lacks the `aria-haspopup="listbox"` attribute. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce that the input controls a popup listbox.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12300: Add `aria-haspopup="listbox"` to the input:
    ```
    setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.HASPOPUP, 'listbox');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing role on listbox
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion listbox (`#search-suggestions`) lacks the `role="listbox"` attribute. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot identify the element as a listbox.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12300: Add `role="listbox"` to the listbox:
    ```
    setAttr(_listEl, ARIA.ROLE, 'listbox');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on listbox
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion listbox (`#search-suggestions`) lacks an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot announce the purpose of the listbox.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12300: Add `aria-label` to the listbox:
    ```
    setAttr(_listEl, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.searchSuggestionsLabel || 'Search suggestions');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing role on listbox options
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion listbox options lack the `role="option"` attribute. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot identify the elements as selectable options.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12300: Add `role="option"` to each option in `_itemHtml`:
    ```
    role: 'option',
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-selected on listbox options
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion listbox options lack the `aria-selected` attribute. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce which option is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12300: Add `aria-selected` to each option in `_itemHtml`:
    ```
    'aria-selected': 'false',
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-labelledby on listbox options
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion listbox options lack the `aria-labelledby` attribute pointing to the option's text content. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot associate the option with its label.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus management in combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The `searchInput` combobox does not manage focus when the suggestion listbox is opened or closed. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as keyboard users may lose focus context.

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard navigation in listbox
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion listbox does not support keyboard navigation (e.g., ArrowUp, ArrowDown, Home, End). This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as keyboard users cannot navigate the suggestions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12300: The `_onInputKeydown` function already handles keyboard navigation, but ensure it is bound to the input:
    ```
    _inputEl.addEventListener('keydown', _onInputKeydown);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on progressbar
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload progress indicators (`<progress>`) lack `aria-label` attributes. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot announce the purpose of the progress bars.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13100: Add `aria-label` to the progress elements in `_renderRow`:
    ```
    'aria-label': `Upload progress for ${entry.file.name}`,
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-valuetext on progressbar
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload progress indicators (`<progress>`) lack `aria-valuetext` attributes. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot announce the progress in a user-friendly way.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13100: Update `aria-valuetext` in the progress event handler:
    ```
    progressEl.setAttribute('aria-valuetext', `${entry.progress}%`);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing role on progressbar
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload progress indicators (`<progress>`) lack `role="progressbar"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot identify the elements as progress bars.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13100: Add `role="progressbar"` to the progress elements in `_renderRow`:
    ```
    role: 'progressbar',
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on tablist
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tabs container (`[data-filter-tabs]`) lacks an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot announce the purpose of the tablist.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10900: Add `aria-label` to the tablist:
    ```
    setAttr(_tabsEl, ARIA.LABEL, 'Product categories');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing role on tablist
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tabs container (`[data-filter-tabs]`) lacks `role="tablist"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot identify the element as a tablist.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10900: Add `role="tablist"` to the tablist:
    ```
    setAttr(_tabsEl, ARIA.ROLE, 'tablist');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing role on tab
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) lack `role="tab"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot identify the elements as tabs.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-selected on tab
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) lack `aria-selected` attributes. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce which tab is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10900: Add `aria-selected` to each tab button in `_syncTabAriaStates`:
    ```
    setAttr(btn, ARIA.SELECTED, String(isActive));
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-controls on tab
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) lack `aria-controls` attributes pointing to the associated tabpanel. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot associate the tab with its content.

issuesFound.title: Missing role on tabpanel
issuesFound.explanation: The product grid container (`[data-product-grid]`) lacks `role="tabpanel"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot identify the element as a tabpanel.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-labelledby on tabpanel

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on carousel

issuesFound.title: Missing role on carousel

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-roledescription on slides
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel slides (`[data-slide]`) lack `aria-roledescription="slide"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot identify the elements as slides.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on slides
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel slides (`[data-slide]`) lack `aria-label` attributes. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot announce the purpose of each slide.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12500: Add `aria-label` to each slide in `_parseSteps`:

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-hidden on inactive slides
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel slides (`[data-slide]`) do not have `aria-hidden="true"` set when they are inactive. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers may announce inactive slides.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12500: Add `aria-hidden="true"` to inactive slides in `goTo`:

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on button
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous (`[data-carousel-prev]`) and next (`[data-carousel-next]`) buttons lack `aria-label` attributes. This violates WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce their purpose.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-pressed on button
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button (`[data-carousel-play]`) lacks `aria-pressed` to indicate its state. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce whether the carousel is playing or paused.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12500: Add `aria-pressed` to the button in `play` and `pause`:

    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing role on button
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button (`[data-carousel-play]`) lacks `role="button"` if it is not a native `<button>`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot identify the element as a button.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus management in modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer does not trap keyboard focus when opened. This violates WCAG 2.1.2 (No Keyboard Trap) as keyboard users may become trapped outside the modal.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus return on modal close
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer does not return focus to the trigger button when closed. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) as keyboard users lose their place in the page.

Line 10500: Return focus to the trigger button in `close`:
    ```
    _trigger.focus();
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-modal on modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer lacks `aria-modal="true"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot identify the element as a modal dialog.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10500: Add `aria-modal="true"` to the drawer:
    ```

    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer lacks an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot announce the purpose of the modal.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10500: Add `aria-label` to the drawer:
    ```
    setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.LABEL, 'Navigation menu');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing role on modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer lacks `role="dialog"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot identify the element as a dialog.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on fieldset
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fieldset lacks an `aria-label` or `<legend>` element. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot announce the purpose of the fieldset.

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete on form field
issuesFound.explanation: The `contactCompany` (`#contact-company`) input in the contact form lacks the `autocomplete` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose) as browsers and assistive technologies cannot programmatically determine the purpose of this field.

    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-describedby on form field
issuesFound.explanation: The `contactMessage` (`#contact-message`) textarea in the contact form lacks an `aria-describedby` attribute pointing to its character counter. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot associate the textarea with its counter.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on iframe
issuesFound.explanation: The `labelIframes` function in `srUtils` does not label iframes that lack `title`, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby` attributes. This violates WCAG 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce the purpose of the iframe.

Line 12900: The function already labels iframes, but ensure it is called during initialisation:

    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing role on alert
issuesFound.explanation: The error message elements created by `inlineValidator` lack `role="alert"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot identify the elements as alerts.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live on alert
issuesFound.explanation: The error message elements created by `inlineValidator` lack `aria-live="assertive"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) as screen readers may not announce the error immediately.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13000: Add `aria-live="assertive"` to the error elements in `_getOrCreateErrorEl`:
    ```
    'aria-live': 'assertive',
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-atomic on alert
issuesFound.explanation: The error message elements created by `inlineValidator` lack `aria-atomic="true"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) as screen readers may not announce the entire message if only part of the content changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13000: Add `aria-atomic="true"` to the error elements in `_getOrCreateErrorEl`:

    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on upload zone
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone (`zoneEl`) lacks an `aria-label` that describes its purpose. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot announce the purpose of the upload zone.

issuesFound.title: Missing role on upload zone
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone (`zoneEl`) lacks `role="button"` if it is not a native `<button>` element. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot identify the element as a button.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13100: Add `role="button"` to the upload zone in `bind`:
    ```
    setAttr(zoneEl, ARIA.ROLE, 'button');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing tabindex on upload zone
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone (`zoneEl`) lacks `tabindex="0"` if it is not a native interactive element. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as keyboard users cannot focus the upload zone.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13100: Add `tabindex="0"` to the upload zone in `bind`:

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler on upload zone
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone (`zoneEl`) lacks keyboard event handlers for `Enter` and `Space` keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as keyboard users cannot activate the upload zone.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-valuenow on progressbar
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard progress bar (`[data-wizard-progress]`) lacks `aria-valuenow` attribute. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce the current progress.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13300: Update `aria-valuenow` in `_updateProgress`:
    ```
    setAttr(_progressBar, ARIA.VALUENOW, String(percent));
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing focus management in wizard
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard does not move focus to the new step panel when navigating between steps. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) as keyboard users lose their place in the form.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-invalid on form field
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input (`#newsletter-email`) does not have `aria-invalid="true"` set when validation fails. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification) as screen readers cannot programmatically determine the field's invalid state.

Line 13400: Set `aria-invalid="true"` in the validation error handler:

issuesFound.title: Missing tabindex on button
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button (`#scroll-top-btn`) lacks `tabindex="0"` if it is not a native interactive element. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as keyboard users cannot focus the button.

Line 10800: Add `tabindex="0"` to the button:

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler on button
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button (`#scroll-top-btn`) lacks keyboard event handlers for `Enter` and `Space` keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as keyboard users cannot activate the button.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link (`.skip-link`) lacks an `aria-label` or visible text content. This violates WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as screen readers cannot announce its purpose.

    setAttr(qs('.skip-link'), ARIA.LABEL, 'Skip to main content');

issuesFound.title: Missing href on skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link (`.skip-link`) lacks an `href` attribute pointing to the main content. This violates WCAG 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks) as the link cannot be activated to skip to the main content.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The `footerNav` (`.footer-inner nav`) lacks an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as screen readers cannot distinguish this navigation from others on the page.
