issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change, leaving keyboard and assistive technology users unaware of the menu's open/closed status.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10053: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
    Line 10060: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in the `pricing` module toggles the billing period between "monthly" and "annual" but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as the button's pressed state is not conveyed to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10850: Add after `const next = _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'month' : 'year';`:
    ```
    setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, String(next === 'year'));
    ```

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function in the `pricing` module updates the displayed prices and billing period labels but does not announce these changes to screen readers via an ARIA live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages), as users relying on assistive technologies are not informed of the dynamic update.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10860: Add after `emit(_rootEl, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`
    ```
    liveRegion.announce(
      period === 'year'
        ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing — save 20%'
        : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'
    );
    ```

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module updates the visual error state of form fields but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute to `true` for invalid fields. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification), as screen readers cannot programmatically identify invalid inputs.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module removes the visual error state for valid fields but does not explicitly set `aria-invalid` to `false` (it only removes the class). While removing the attribute would also work, explicitly setting it to `false` ensures consistency and clarity for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `mobileNav` module does not initialise the `aria-expanded` attribute on the navigation toggle button (`_trigger`) to `false` when the page loads. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as the initial state of the navigation drawer is not programmatically conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10100: Add after `_initialised = true;`:
    ```
    setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module are triggered by click events on the navigation toggle button (`_trigger`), but there is no corresponding `keydown` handler for `Enter` or `Space` keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard), as keyboard users cannot activate the toggle.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content injected via innerHTML without adjacent ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `render` function in the `suggestionListRenderer` module injects HTML into the suggestion list using `innerHTML` but does not update an adjacent ARIA live region to announce the new suggestions to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages), as dynamic content changes are not conveyed to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: setTimeout changing visible content without pause/stop control
issuesFound.explanation: The `play` function in the `testimonialsCarousel` module uses `setTimeout` to auto-advance slides without providing a user-controllable pause/stop mechanism. This violates WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide), as users cannot control the timing of the auto-rotating content.

issuesFound.title: focus() not called on modal/dialog open
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `mobileNav` module shows the navigation drawer but does not programmatically move focus into the drawer. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order), as keyboard users are not automatically placed inside the newly opened menu, making navigation difficult or impossible.

issuesFound.title: focus() not returned to trigger on modal/dialog close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in the `mobileNav` module hides the navigation drawer but does not return focus to the trigger button (`_trigger`). This violates WCAG 2.4.3, as keyboard users lose their place in the focus order after closing the menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10060: Add after `removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`:
    ```
    setTimeout(() => _trigger.focus(), 100);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`SELECTORS.searchInput`) in the `siteSearch` module lacks an accessible name. While it has a `placeholder` attribute, this is not sufficient for accessibility, as placeholders disappear when the input is filled and are not reliably announced by all screen readers. This violates WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) and 3.3.2 (Labels or Instructions).

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for contact form inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form inputs (`contactFirstName`, `contactLastName`, `contactEmail`, `contactMessage`, etc.) in `SELECTORS` lack accessible names. While they may have `placeholder` attributes, these are not sufficient for accessibility. This violates WCAG 3.3.2, as screen reader users cannot reliably determine the purpose of each input.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for cookie consent banner
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner (`[data-cookie-banner]`) lacks an accessible name. It has a heading but no `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute to programmatically associate the heading with the banner. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen reader users may not understand the banner's purpose.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons (`[data-filter-value]` in `SELECTORS.filterTabs`) in the `productFilter` module lack accessible names. While they may have visible text, this text is not programmatically associated with the buttons using `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen reader users cannot reliably determine the purpose of each tab.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for wizard navigation buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard navigation buttons (`[data-wizard-prev]`, `[data-wizard-next]`, `[data-wizard-submit]`) in the `multiStepWizard` module lack accessible names. While they may have visible text, this text is not programmatically associated with the buttons using `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen reader users cannot reliably determine the purpose of each button.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-required for required form fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` module validates required form fields but does not set the `aria-required` attribute to `true` on these fields. This violates WCAG 3.3.2, as screen reader users are not informed that the fields are required before submission.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-describedby for form fields with error messages
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` module inserts error messages adjacent to invalid form fields but does not associate them with the fields using `aria-describedby`. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification), as screen reader users cannot programmatically associate error messages with the corresponding inputs.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live region for form submission status
issuesFound.explanation: The `newsletterForm` and `contactForm` modules update the form status (success/error messages) but do not use an ARIA live region to announce these changes to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3, as users are not informed of the dynamic status updates.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live region for wizard step changes
issuesFound.explanation: The `multiStepWizard` module advances through form steps but does not announce these changes to screen readers via an ARIA live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3, as users are not informed of the dynamic step changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12600: Add to the `_goToStep` function (line 12630) after `emit(_form, 'wizard:step', { step: _current, title: entering.title });`:
    ```
    liveRegion.announce(
      (STRINGS.wizardStepChanged || 'Step {n} of {total}: {title}')
        .replace('{n}', String(_current + 1))
        .replace('{total}', String(_steps.length))
        .replace('{title}', entering.title)
    );
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live region for product filter count updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter` module updates the product count badge (`_countBadge`) when filters are applied but does not announce these changes to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3, as users are not informed of the dynamic count updates.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 9700: Add to the `applyFilters` function (line 9720) after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`:
    ```
    liveRegion.announce(
      totalMatches === 0
        ? STRINGS.filterNoResults || 'No products match your filters'
        : (STRINGS.filterResultsCount || '{n} product{s} match your filters')
            .replace('{n}', String(totalMatches))
            .replace('{s}', totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : '')
    );
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live region for search results count
issuesFound.explanation: The `siteSearch` module updates the search results count but does not announce these changes to screen readers via an ARIA live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3, as users are not informed of the dynamic count updates.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="status" for pricing savings message
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateSavingsMessage` function in the `pricing` module updates the savings message (`_savingsMsg`) but does not assign `role="status"` to the element. This violates WCAG 4.1.3, as screen readers may not announce the dynamic savings information.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10800: Add to the `_updateSavingsMessage` function (line 10830) after `_savingsMsg.textContent = ...`:
    ```
    setAttr(_savingsMsg, 'role', 'status');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing role="alert" for form error summary
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderErrorSummary` function in the `contactForm` module creates an error summary box but does not assign `role="alert"` to the container. This violates WCAG 3.3.1, as screen readers may not immediately announce the error summary to users.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-atomic="true" for live regions
issuesFound.explanation: The `liveRegion` module creates ARIA live regions but does not set `aria-atomic="true"` on the polite and assertive containers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3, as screen readers may not read the entire message if only part of the live region changes.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-relevant="additions text" for live regions
issuesFound.explanation: The `liveRegion` module creates ARIA live regions but does not set `aria-relevant="additions text"` on the containers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3, as screen readers may not reliably announce changes to the live region content.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-controls for combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`SELECTORS.searchInput`) in the `siteSearch` module is assigned `role="combobox"` but does not have its `aria-controls` attribute set to the ID of the suggestion list. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen reader users cannot programmatically associate the combobox with its listbox.
issuesFound.fix: Line 9400: The `mount` function in `suggestionListRenderer` (line 9480) already sets `aria-controls` on the input. Ensure the listbox has a unique ID and the input's `aria-controls` references it.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="listbox" for suggestion list
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion list in the `siteSearch` module is created with `role="listbox"` in the `mount` function, but this role is not consistently applied if the list is pre-rendered in the HTML. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen reader users may not recognize the list as a listbox.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="option" for suggestion items
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion items in the `siteSearch` module are created with `role="option"` in the `_itemHtml` function, but this role is not applied if the items are pre-rendered in the HTML. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen reader users may not recognize the items as selectable options.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for listbox
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion list in the `siteSearch` module is created with `aria-label="Search suggestions"` in the `mount` function, but this label is not applied if the list is pre-rendered in the HTML. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen reader users may not understand the purpose of the listbox.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-multiselectable for listbox
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion list in the `siteSearch` module is a single-select listbox but does not have `aria-multiselectable="false"` explicitly set. While this is the default, explicitly setting it improves clarity for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 9500: Add to the `mount` function in `suggestionListRenderer` (line 9480) after `setAttr(_listEl, 'role', 'listbox');`:
    ```
    setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-multiselectable', 'false');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-valuetext for progressbar
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard progress bar (`[data-wizard-progress]`) in the `multiStepWizard` module updates `aria-valuenow` but does not set `aria-valuetext` to a human-readable value (e.g., "Step 2 of 5"). This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen reader users may not receive a meaningful announcement of the progress.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="tabpanel" for wizard steps
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard step panels (`[data-wizard-step]`) in the `multiStepWizard` module lack `role="tabpanel"` attributes. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen reader users may not recognize the panels as tabpanel elements.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-labelledby for tabpanel
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard step panels (`[data-wizard-step]`) in the `multiStepWizard` module have `role="tabpanel"` but lack `aria-labelledby` attributes referencing their associated tab elements. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen reader users cannot programmatically associate the tabpanel with its tab.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12600: This requires creating tab elements (not present in the current implementation). As a workaround, ensure each tabpanel has an `aria-label` (already fixed in Issue 65).

issuesFound.title: Missing role="tab" for wizard navigation buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard navigation buttons (`[data-wizard-prev]`, `[data-wizard-next]`) in the `multiStepWizard` module are not assigned `role="tab"`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen reader users may not recognize the buttons as part of a tabbed interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12600: The current implementation does not use a tablist/tabpanel pattern for navigation buttons. Instead, ensure the buttons have clear accessible names (already fixed in Issue 36).

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-orientation for tablist
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu in the `dropdownMenu` module uses a container with `role="tablist"` but does not set `aria-orientation="horizontal"`. While this is the default, explicitly setting it improves clarity for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-hidden for decorative icons
issuesFound.explanation: Decorative icons (e.g., in suggestion items, carousel buttons, pricing toggle) are not marked with `aria-hidden="true"`. This violates WCAG 1.1.1 (Non-text Content), as screen readers may announce irrelevant icon text.

issuesFound.title: Missing alt text for decorative images
issuesFound.explanation: Decorative images (e.g., in testimonial cards, feature sections) are not consistently marked with `alt=""`. This violates WCAG 1.1.1, as screen readers may announce irrelevant image filenames.

issuesFound.title: Missing lang attribute for non-English text
issuesFound.explanation: The application supports localisation but does not dynamically update the `<html lang>` attribute when the locale changes (e.g., `CONFIG.app.locale`). This violates WCAG 3.1.1 (Language of Page), as screen readers may not switch to the correct language pronunciation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 100: Add to the `app.init` function (line 13800) after `cookieConsent.init();`:
    ```
    document.documentElement.lang = CONFIG.app.locale || 'en-GB';
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-busy for loading states
issuesFound.explanation: The `siteSearch`, `newsletterForm`, and `contactForm` modules show loading states (e.g., during form submission or search) but do not set `aria-busy="true"` on the relevant containers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3, as screen reader users are not informed of the loading state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 9400: Add to the `_fetchAndRender` function in `siteSearch` (line 9450) after `_searchState.loading = true;`:
    ```
    srUtils.setBusy(_inputEl.parentElement, true);
    ```
    And after `_searchState.loading = false;`:
    ```
    srUtils.setBusy(_inputEl.parentElement, false);
    ```
    Line 12300: Add to the `_onSubmit` function in `newsletterForm` (line 12350) after `_submitting = true;`:
    ```
    srUtils.setBusy(_formEl, true);
    ```
    And after `_submitting = false;`:
    ```
    srUtils.setBusy(_formEl, false);
    ```
    Line 12400: Add to the `_onSubmit` function in `contactForm` (line 12470) after `_submitting = true;`:
    ```
    srUtils.setBusy(_formEl, true);
    ```
    And after `_submitting = false;`:
    ```
    srUtils.setBusy(_formEl, false);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live for character counter
issuesFound.explanation: The character counter in the `characterCounter` module updates the remaining character count but does not use an ARIA live region to announce these changes to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3, as users are not informed of the dynamic count updates.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for iframe
issuesFound.explanation: The `labelIframes` function in `srUtils` assigns a generic `title` to iframes but does not use `aria-label` for more descriptive labels. This violates WCAG 2.4.4 (Link Purpose) and 4.1.2, as screen reader users may not understand the purpose of the iframe.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13500: Replace in the `labelIframes` function (line 13520):
    ```
    iframe.setAttribute('title', `Content frame ${idx + 1}`);
    ```
    with:
    ```
    iframe.setAttribute('aria-label', `Content frame ${idx + 1}`);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for skip links
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links (`SELECTORS.skipLinks`) lack `aria-label` attributes to describe their purpose (e.g., "Skip to main content"). This violates WCAG 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks), as screen reader users may not understand the purpose of the links.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-current for active nav links
issuesFound.explanation: The `activeNavLinks` module sets `aria-current="page"` on active navigation links, but this attribute is not consistently applied if the links are pre-rendered with an active state. This violates WCAG 1.3.1, as screen reader users may not recognize the active link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 8800: The `init` function in `activeNavLinks` (line 8820) already sets `aria-current`. Ensure it is called on page load.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-current for active filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter` module sets a CSS class (`is-active`) on active filter tabs but does not set `aria-current="true"`. This violates WCAG 1.3.1, as screen reader users may not recognize the active tab.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-current for active view mode button
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter` module sets `aria-pressed` on view mode toggle buttons but does not set `aria-current="true"` on the active button. While `aria-pressed` is valid, `aria-current` is more semantically appropriate for view mode toggles. This violates WCAG 1.3.1.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-selected for active suggestion item
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer` module updates the `aria-selected` attribute on suggestion items when the active index changes, but this attribute is not initialised for the first item when the list is rendered. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen reader users may not recognize the initially highlighted item.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-hidden for decorative separators
issuesFound.explanation: Decorative separators (e.g., in breadcrumbs, navigation menus) are not marked with `aria-hidden="true"`. This violates WCAG 1.1.1, as screen readers may announce irrelevant separator characters.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-hidden for non-essential icons
issuesFound.explanation: Non-essential icons (e.g., in carousel buttons, pricing toggle) are not consistently marked with `aria-hidden="true"`. This violates WCAG 1.1.1, as screen readers may announce irrelevant icon text.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="img" for SVG icons
issuesFound.explanation: The `processSvgIcons` function in `srUtils` assigns `role="img"` to SVGs with accessible names, but this role is not applied if the SVG is pre-rendered in the HTML. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen reader users may not recognize the SVG as an image.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus styles for keyboard navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The application does not consistently apply visible focus styles to interactive elements when navigated via keyboard. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible), as keyboard users may lose track of their focus position.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus trap for modal dialogs
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav`, `dropdownMenu`, and `faqAccordion` modules open modal-like content (navigation drawer, dropdown panels, FAQ answers) but do not implement a focus trap to constrain keyboard navigation within the modal. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order), as keyboard users may accidentally navigate outside the modal.

issuesFound.title: Missing reduced motion guard for animations
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav`, `dropdownMenu`, `faqAccordion`, and `testimonialsCarousel` modules use CSS transitions/animations but do not consistently check `prefers-reduced-motion` before applying them. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions), as users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_onTriggerClick` (lines 10234–10249) handles click events on dropdown triggers but does not provide a matching keyboard handler for Enter/Space keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) because non-native interactive elements (div/span buttons) must be operable via keyboard. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot open dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a keydown handler to the same container that listens for Enter/Space keys and calls the same toggle logic as the click handler.
  Line 10234: `container.addEventListener('keydown', function _onTriggerKeydown(e) {`
  Line 10235: `  if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) {`
  Line 10236: `    e.preventDefault();`
  Line 10237: `    _onTriggerClick(e);`
  Line 10238: `  }`
  Line 10239: `});`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_updateCountBadge` (lines 10700–10715) updates the visible product count badge but does not announce the change to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) because users who rely on screen readers cannot perceive the updated count.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a live region announcement immediately after updating the badge text.
  Line 10715: `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} visible`);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_validateAll` (lines 12200–12299) in the contact form marks fields as invalid by adding an error class and inserting an error message but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid fields. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) because screen readers cannot announce the invalid state. Users who rely on screen readers cannot perceive which fields contain errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after pushing the error to the errors array.
  Line 12215: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_validateAll` (lines 12200–12299) does not clear `aria-invalid="false"` when a field becomes valid. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) because screen readers continue to announce the field as invalid even after correction. Users who rely on screen readers cannot perceive that the error has been fixed.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` immediately after determining that the field is valid (i.e., when no errors are pushed).
  Line 12210: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.explanation: Social media icons in the footer are decorative and rendered as spans with class "social-icon". These icons are purely visual and should be hidden from assistive technology to avoid confusion.

Line 1502: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="social-icon" />`

issuesFound.title: Navigation landmark missing distinguishing label

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple selectors set `outline: none` on `:focus-visible` without defining a replacement visible focus style (such as `border`, `box-shadow`, or `outline-color`). This makes keyboard focus invisible for users navigating via keyboard, blocking access to interactive elements.

issuesFound.title: Visually hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, and `clip: auto`, which keeps elements visible on screen and in layout. This defeats the purpose of a screen-reader-only utility, potentially cluttering the visual interface or failing to hide sensitive content from sighted users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 320: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Several elements (`.skip-link`, `.btn`, `.hero-ctas .btn-primary`, `.logo-list img`, `.form-group input`, `.social-links img`) define `transition` properties that cause movement or visual change, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block at line 335 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience these animations.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`. Negative word spacing reduces the space between words, which can cause text to appear crowded and significantly degrade readability for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 137: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Base font size set below readable minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` element sets `font-size: 12px`. Since `body` text uses `rem` units based on this root, all body text renders at 12px, which is below the recommended 16px (1rem) minimum for readability, affecting users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 113: html { font-size: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative indicator

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, and `clip: auto`, which leaves content visible on the screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it available to screen readers. This exposes helper text or skip links that should be hidden (WCAG 1.3.1).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary buttons

This fails the minimum 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3). Affects `.btn-primary`, `.btn-ghost`, and `.btn-outline`.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in footer
issuesFound.explanation: Footer text uses `#94a3b8` (var(--colour-neutral-400)) on a background of `#555555` (var(--colour-neutral-900)). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.2:1, which is significantly below the 4.5:1 minimum (WCAG 1.4.3).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (Icon Button)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-icon` class sets `min-width` and `min-height` to 20px. WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 requires a minimum target size of 24x24 pixels for interactive elements to assist users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 463: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size (Checkbox)
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to 20x20 pixels (`width: 20px; height: 20px;`). This is below the 24px minimum target size required by WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.skip-link` element has a `transition: transform` defined, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block at line 417 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the slide-in animation (WCAG 2.3.3).
issuesFound.fix: Line 417: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`. Negative word spacing can cause characters to collide or become illegible for users with cognitive disabilities or when text is zoomed (WCAG 1.4.12).
issuesFound.fix: Line 153: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have `text-decoration: none` and only change color on hover. Relying on color alone to distinguish links from surrounding text fails WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color), especially for users with color blindness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1173: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-200); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA).

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Users navigating by keyboard cannot see when buttons receive focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA).

issuesFound.title: Logo link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing an alternative visual indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the logo link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a replacement. This mobile menu button will not show a focus indicator to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Submenu link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Dropdown menu links will not show focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Search input focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a replacement. Although a box-shadow is defined for :focus, the :focus-visible rule explicitly sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0, potentially overriding the visual indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Search button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. The search submit button will not show a focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a replacement. Account navigation links will not show focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. The "Learn more" links in feature cards will not show focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a replacement. Filter buttons will not show focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a replacement. The monthly/annual toggle buttons will not show focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a replacement. The scrollable table region will not show a focus indicator when focused.

issuesFound.title: FAQ button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a replacement. FAQ accordion buttons will not show focus indicators.

issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input:focus-visible, select:focus-visible, textarea:focus-visible selectors remove the outline without a replacement. Form fields will not show focus indicators.

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property, which can cause motion animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.

issuesFound.title: Skip link transition missing reduced motion override

issuesFound.title: Hero image transition missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 892 is empty and does not override the transform transition defined for .hero-image img at line 905. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the 3D perspective animation.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which is a negative value. This reduces the space between words, making it harder for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities to distinguish between words and read the content.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border-color change. Keyboard users navigating the page will not see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 353: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default outline with outline: none and only sets outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users on button elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size minimum of 24×24 pixels. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these small buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 598: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24×24 pixel minimum target size required by WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 610: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24×24 pixel minimum target size, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to select.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1532: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which meets the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24px but falls below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size for optimal accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 535: `.btn { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); ... }`

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-lg class sets min-height: 36px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size.

issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, meeting the 24px minimum but below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size.

issuesFound.explanation: Navigation menu items set min-height: 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 748: `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); ... }`

issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set min-height: 28px, below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 783: `[id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); ... }`

issuesFound.explanation: The search input sets height: 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 802: `#search-input { height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets width: 28px and height: 28px, below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size.

issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons set min-height: 28px, below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size.

issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons set min-height: 28px, below the recommended 44×44 pixel target size.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and visible overflow, which makes the content visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended functionality for screen-reader-only text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 377: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn transform transition
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, which can cause motion sensitivity issues. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: Keep existing transition. Add after line 537: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }` (removing transform)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn:active transform
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active selector applies a translateY transform without a prefers-reduced-motion override, which may affect users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 553: Wrap in media query: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) { .btn:active { transform: translateY(1px); } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: hero CTA button hover transform
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button hover applies a translateY transform without respecting prefers-reduced-motion, potentially causing issues for motion-sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 891: Wrap in media query: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img transform transition
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image includes transform in its transition and applies perspective transforms on hover without a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 927: Add to existing empty media query at line 935: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-slower); transform: none !important; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card--featured:hover transform
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card hover applies translateY and scale transforms that are not covered by the existing prefers-reduced-motion media query (which only targets .pricing-card:hover).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1197: Add to media query at line 1194: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link transform transition
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link has a transform transition that is not disabled in the empty prefers-reduced-motion media query at line 439, potentially affecting motion-sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 439: Add to empty media query: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 338: Remove word-spacing declaration or change to `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .stat-label
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% opacity white) against a gradient background of #5588bb to #bb88ee. The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 2.1:1, which fails WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum) of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1234: `.stat-label { color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-color visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight) in the default state. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color, as links are only distinguishable by color from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 188: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` OR add `a { border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; text-decoration: none; }`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show and hide the dropdown panels by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but they do not synchronise the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1493: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` at the start of _openInstance
  Line 1505: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the start of _closeInstance

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open() and _close() functions manage the visibility of the suggestion listbox but never update aria-expanded on the combobox input. Since the input has role="combobox", it must have aria-expanded set to "true" when the listbox is displayed and "false" when it is hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2073: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside _open after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line 2085: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside _close after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the FAQ answer panel and updates the visual state, but unlike the close() function, it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves the accessible name in an inconsistent state where the panel is visible but the button still reports itself as collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2636: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside the open function after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons that activate/deactivate filter categories, but the _syncTabAriaStates function only toggles CSS classes. It does not set aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state, which is required for toggle buttons to communicate their active status to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) function as a single-select toggle group where one button is always active. The _syncToggleState function updates CSS classes but does not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button or aria-pressed="false" on inactive buttons.

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the _updateCountBadge function updates the text content of the count badge to reflect the number of visible products, but the badge element is not an ARIA live region. Screen reader users receive no notification when the result count changes dynamically.

issuesFound.title: pricing price changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched, the price values are updated visually (with animation), but there is no announcement to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed that the prices have changed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets up aria-haspopup and aria-controls on trigger buttons, but it does not initialise aria-expanded="false". Since the panel is initially hidden (panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')), the trigger must reflect this state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1580: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add:
  ```
  setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');
  ```

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount() function configures the search input as a combobox with aria-haspopup="listbox", but it does not initialise aria-expanded="false". The combobox pattern requires this attribute to indicate the initial collapsed state of the suggestions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1993: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add:
  ```
  setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');
  ```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3078 (in open function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`. Line 3098 (in close function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function declares a _trap variable but never instantiates or activates a focus trap. When the drawer opens, keyboard focus can escape to background elements, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3082 (in open function, after _lockScroll): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls on the trigger button, but does not initialize aria-expanded="false". Since the panel is hidden by default, screen readers incorrectly report the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3248 (after panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')): Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function toggles visual active states for billing period buttons (month/year/quarter) but does not set aria-pressed. These buttons function as toggle buttons (one active at a time), so screen readers cannot convey which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4675 (inside forEach): After `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher buttons missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher.init function applies the saved theme preference but does not initialize aria-pressed on the theme toggle buttons. Screen readers cannot indicate which theme (light/dark/system) is currently active on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5745 (after _apply(_resolve(_current))): Add `qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => { const btnTheme = btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle'); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btnTheme === _current)); });`.

issuesFound.title: Price changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes (monthly to annual), the pricing.setPeriod function updates price values visually but does not announce the change via the live region. Screen reader users are not notified that prices have changed across all plan cards.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4630 (after planDataParser.getAll().forEach(...)): Add `liveRegion.announce('Prices switched to ' + BILLING_PERIODS[period].label + ' billing');`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function adds visual error styling and updates aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields. Screen readers cannot convey the validation state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5405 (in else block for invalid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. Line 5410 (in if block for valid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Wizard progress bar missing aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax
issuesFound.explanation: The multiStepWizard._updateProgress function sets aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext on the progress bar element, but does not set aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax. Screen readers cannot calculate the percentage completion without these attributes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5525 (after getting _progressBar): Add `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0');` and `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100');` (or set once in init).

issuesFound.title: Dynamic price updates missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module updates price text content when billing periods change, but these changes are not announced to screen readers because the price elements do not use aria-live regions and the code does not call liveRegion.announce.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4630 (in setPeriod): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingChanged || 'Prices updated to ' + BILLING_PERIODS[period].label + ' billing');`.

fpTitles: Wizard progress bar missing aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu instances open and close panels without updating aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. Assistive technologies cannot announce the state change to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3654: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 3669: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: Form validation errors are visually indicated but the aria-invalid attribute is not set to reflect the validation state. Screen readers cannot convey whether fields are valid or invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5124: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, String(!result.valid));

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false". The panel is hidden but the trigger state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3704: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: productFilter count updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge updates visually but the change is not announced to screen readers because the element lacks aria-live.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4556: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without user control
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after a timeout without providing users a way to pause, stop, or extend the duration. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3283: (remove entire if block) if (dur > 0) { timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur); }

fpTitles: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without user control

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop to provide an accessible name for screen reader users, but the close button in the mobile navigation panel renders only the multiplication symbol (✕) without an aria-label. Icon-only buttons must have a text alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1648: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel's previous slide button uses the IconButton component but does not provide an aria-label prop. The button contains only the character "‹", which may not be announced meaningfully by all screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2700: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel's next slide button uses the IconButton component without an aria-label prop. The button contains only the character "›", leaving screen reader users without context for the button's action.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: Base font-size below recommended minimum

issuesFound.title: Line-height below minimum on paragraph elements

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This affects all focusable elements on the page, making keyboard focus invisible for users who rely on visual focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 272-275: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when a button has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 378-380: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link focus state removes outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when the logo link has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447-449: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .nav-toggle

issuesFound.fix: Line 467-469: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: In forced-colors (high contrast) mode, the :focus-visible rule removes outline without providing a visible alternative. Users in high contrast mode cannot see keyboard focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 282-285: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class is completely broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses properties that make content VISIBLE instead of hiding it. Position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto are the opposite of what a screen-reader-only class should use. Content meant to be hidden from sighted users will be fully visible, and content meant to be announced by screen readers may not be properly hidden from view.
issuesFound.fix: Line 299-308: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons have min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24x24 pixels. Users with motor impairments will have difficulty activating these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 427-430: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are set to 20x20 pixels, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24x24 pixels. Users with motor impairments will have difficulty selecting checkboxes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1239: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: Stat labels use rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% opacity white) on a gradient background of #5588bb to #bb88ee. This results in approximately 2:1 contrast ratio, far below the required 4.5:1 for normal text. Text is unreadable for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 899-904: .stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Root font-size set below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, below the recommended 16px (1rem) baseline. This causes all rem-based typography to render smaller than intended, reducing readability for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 234: html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 1rem; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height too tight for readability
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraphs have line-height: 1, which is too tight for readable body text. WCAG Success Criterion 1.4.12 recommends a minimum line-height of 1.5 for body text to ensure readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 263: p { line-height: var(--line-height-normal); text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em, which compresses text spacing below normal. Negative spacing reduces readability, particularly for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 236: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; }

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without non-colour visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor rule sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. No other non-colour visual differentiator (border-bottom, font-weight change, background highlight) is provided, meaning links are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 258: a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; } or Line 258: a { text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform transitions but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable or reduce this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience transform animations on button interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 387: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion block fails to disable hero image animation
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img contains empty rules, failing to disable the transform transition defined on line 658-661. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the 3D rotation animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 668-673: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for product cards
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card:hover rule applies transform: translateY(-3px) animation but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 859: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Small buttons (.btn-sm) have min-height: 20px, which with padding results in approximately 32px total height. This is below the recommended 44x44 pixel touch target size, making activation difficult for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 417-420: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle has min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, below the recommended 44x44 pixel touch target. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the menu toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 462-470: .nav-toggle { display: none; min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: var(--space-2); background: none; border: 1px solid var(--colour-border); border-radius: var(--radius-md); color: var(--colour-text-primary); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast); }
