issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, aria-invalid="true" is never set on the input element. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field becomes valid, aria-invalid is not cleared. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid even after correction.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() does not announce filter changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When products are filtered, the visible count changes and cards are shown/hidden, but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has changed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu has opened, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2875: After `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav close function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function removes CSS classes to hide the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2895: After `removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the answer panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Although initialization sets aria-expanded, the dynamic open function fails to update it, leaving screen reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5408: After `item.open = true;`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-pressed attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() function toggles CSS classes on filter buttons but never sets aria-pressed. Filter buttons acting as toggle controls must expose their pressed state to screen readers via aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4243: Replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function links the field to the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2383: After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid reset on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the error message reference but does not explicitly set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. While some assistive technologies may infer validity, explicit state is required for consistent screen reader behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2395: After the existing aria-describedby handling, add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function dynamically shows/hides product cards based on filter selection but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users of assistive technology will not be informed that content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4205: After `_updateEmptyState(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) || 'Showing ' + totalMatches + ' products');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates all price displays when the billing period changes but does not announce this change. Screen reader users will not be informed that prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4715: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the accordion panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state where opening an item leaves aria-expanded at its initial value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4123: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function toggles the CSS active class on filter buttons but never updates aria-pressed. Filter buttons acting as toggle controls must communicate their pressed state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3599: add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle element is assigned role="switch" but aria-checked is never updated when the billing period changes. Switch roles require aria-checked to communicate their state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3895: add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));` after `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear);`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is changed, the visible prices update but there is no live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed that prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3876: add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));` after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult() function adds/removes CSS classes and updates error messages but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid inputs or aria-invalid="false" on valid inputs. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine field validity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4652: add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);` and Line 4658: add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls on dropdown triggers but never initializes aria-expanded="false". Dropdowns start hidden but the trigger's state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2344: add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function sets up event listeners but never initializes aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button. The initial collapsed state is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2199: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not manage focus when drawer opens
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not moved into the drawer. Keyboard users may be left focused on the trigger button outside the drawer, making it difficult to navigate the newly opened content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2119: add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` after `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not return focus to trigger when drawer closes
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the navigation order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2147: add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`

fpTitles: mobileNav does not manage focus when drawer opens

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop and applies error styling, but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine that the field has a validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 556: `<input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop and applies error styling, but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the textarea element. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine that the field has a validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 574: `<textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop and applies error styling, but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the select element. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine that the field has a validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 595: `<select id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing required aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton with only an emoji icon (☀️, 🌙, or 💻) but does not pass the required aria-label prop. The LABELS object defining accessible names is defined but never used. Screen reader users hear only the emoji character with no context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1052: `<IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} aria-label={LABELS[theme]} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing required aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The previous button in the testimonials carousel uses IconButton with only a ‹ character as content, but does not pass the required aria-label prop. Screen reader users cannot understand the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2070: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing required aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The next button in the testimonials carousel uses IconButton with only a › character as content, but does not pass the required aria-label prop. Screen reader users cannot understand the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2075: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link with the nav-link--current class indicates the current page visually but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 900: `<a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link with the mobile-nav__link--current class indicates the current page visually but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1005: `<a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute placed on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div, but it should be on the element with role="dialog". This prevents screen readers from correctly understanding the modal's boundaries.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2725: `<div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>`
  Fix: Line 2728: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component has an isLoading state that shows a spinner and disables the button, but does not set aria-busy="true" to communicate the loading state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 532: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The btn-spinner span is purely decorative (the loading text is provided via VisuallyHidden), but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may announce the spinner element redundantly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: `<span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron arrow character (▲/▼) inside the NavDropdown trigger button is decorative—the expanded state should be communicated via aria-expanded—but lacks aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce the symbol.
issuesFound.fix: Line 825: `{isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}`

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav element missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a nav element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this navigation region from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 470: `<nav aria-label="Skip navigation links">`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav element missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders a nav element with id="main-nav" but no aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page, screen reader users benefit from a descriptive accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 890: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav nav element missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav component renders a nav element with className "mobile-nav__body" but no aria-label. Screen reader users cannot distinguish this navigation region from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 970: `<nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">`

issuesFound.title: StatItem stat value missing accessible label for formatted numbers
issuesFound.explanation: The stat value displays formatted numbers like "40,000+" but the accessible valueLabel ("40,000 plus") is defined in the data but never exposed to screen readers. Users may hear ambiguous announcements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1201: `

{stat.value}

`

issuesFound.title: SearchInput has redundant aria-label overriding visible label
issuesFound.explanation: The SearchInput component has both a VisuallyHidden label ("Search Luminary Studio") and an aria-label ("Search") on the input. The aria-label overrides the associated label, creating inconsistency and redundancy.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker asterisk missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required field asterisk (*) is rendered as visible text without aria-hidden="true". While VisuallyHidden provides "(required)" for screen readers, the asterisk may still be announced as "star" by some assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 490: ` *`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button has aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the submenu is open or closed, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: MobileNav toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button changes its aria-label based on state but lacks aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine whether the mobile menu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: IconButton in MobileNav close button missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop by its TypeScript interface, but this instance provides only an onClick handler and className. Screen reader users will hear no accessible name for this close button.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 903: <IconButton
  onClick={onClose}
  aria-label="Close navigation menu"
  className="mobile-nav__close"
>
  ✕
</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel prev/next buttons missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next navigation buttons in the testimonials carousel are IconButtons without aria-label props. Screen reader users cannot identify the purpose of these buttons.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1522: <IconButton
  aria-label="Previous testimonial"
  aria-controls={trackId}
  onClick={handlePrev}
  className="testimonials-track__prev"
>

</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput's aria-describedby attribute references 'newsletter-email-error' when there is an error, but no element with that ID exists in the DOM. The FormGroup component requires errorId and errorMessage props to render the ErrorMessage, but NewsletterForm passes neither. Screen reader users cannot access the error message.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1708: <FormGroup
  label="Email address"
  htmlFor="newsletter-email"
  errorId="newsletter-email-error"
  errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
  required
>
  Line 1726: aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: ContactForm inputs aria-describedby references non-existent IDs
issuesFound.explanation: Each form input in ContactForm references an error ID (contact-name-error, contact-email-error, contact-subject-error, contact-message-error) via aria-describedby, but these IDs do not exist. The FormGroup component requires errorId and errorMessage props to render error messages, but ContactForm passes only an error prop that FormGroup does not recognize. Screen reader users cannot access field-level error messages.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not currently visible remain in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users may encounter all slides simultaneously rather than navigating through them sequentially, confusing the user experience.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal placed on overlay instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div, but it should be on the element with role="dialog". Screen readers may not properly announce the modal's boundaries to users.

issuesFound.title: NotificationContainer has aria-live="off" preventing announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The notification container has aria-live="off", which prevents screen readers from announcing dynamically added notifications. Users will not be informed of important status changes, errors, or success messages.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1913: <div
  className="notification-container"
  aria-label="Notifications"
>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The second <ul> in IntegrationsBar is a visual clone for seamless animation looping but contains the same content as the first list. Without aria-hidden="true", screen reader users will hear all integration items announced twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1066: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component has an isLoading state that shows a spinner and disables the button, but it does not communicate aria-busy="true" to screen readers. Users cannot programmatically determine that the button is in a loading state.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links with item.current=true receive a visual indicator (nav-link--current class) but lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify the current page link.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Mobile navigation links with item.current=true receive a visual indicator but lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify the current page link in mobile navigation.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker asterisk missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required field asterisk (*) is rendered as visible text without aria-hidden="true", while a VisuallyHidden component provides "(required)" text. Screen readers may announce both "*" and "(required)", creating redundant information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 622: *
